title: geum macrophyllum
intro: Geum macrophyllum, also known as the tall avens or tall geum, is a perennial plant species in the rose family (Rosaceae) native to the western United States and Canada. Traditionally, the plant's roots, leaves, and stems have been used in herbal medicine, showcasing antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in soothing digestive issues and reducing fever. The medicinal properties of Geum macrophyllum are also believed to offer various health benefits, including antioxidant and antiseptic effects, which may help to protect against cell damage and promote wound healing. Geum macrophyllum can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although its safety and efficacy as a medicinal plant require further scientific study.
